name: Demo Student
research_background: I develop machine learning methods for problems in structural biology and genomics. My undergraduate research focused on applying transformer-based architectures to protein sequence classification, achieving state-of-the-art performance on fold recognition benchmarks. I also contributed to a project using contrastive self-supervised learning for multi-omics data integration across single-cell RNA-seq and ATAC-seq modalities. I have hands-on experience with PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, and standard bioinformatics tools (kallisto, STAR, Seurat). My senior thesis developed a graph neural network approach for predicting protein-protein interaction networks from sequence embeddings. I am most excited about problems where ML can directly accelerate biological discovery rather than treating biology as a pure benchmark domain.
